reasoning: The evidence is overwhelming that Francis Suarez did not announce a candidacy for Florida governor before July 1, 2025. In the final weeks of June 2025, Suarez was actively focused on municipal legislation to move Miami municipal elections from odd to even years, a move that would have extended his mayoral term into 2026. He signed this measure into law on June 26, 2025. It is highly inconsistent for a politician to actively legislate to extend his mayoral term while simultaneously launching a statewide campaign for governor. Furthermore, reports indicate that when this effort to extend his term was eventually abandoned by September 2025, Suarez formally ruled out a run for governor. He subsequently left office at the end of 2025 and took jobs in the private sector and media (joining Fox News and Nexar's board) in early 2026. Mentions of Suarez in May 2025 polling simply reflect his status as a termed-out mayor and potential candidate, not an officially declared one. There is no record of an announcement before the July 1, 2025 deadline.
